input.background: Evaluate the correctness of hypothesis statements and identify issues with their formulation.
input.question: What is wrong with the hypothesis statement $H_0: \mu=100$ vs. $H_a: \mu \neq 110$?

output.answer: The null and alternative hypotheses must be set up so that either $H_0$ or $H_a$ is true. Here, it is possible for neither to be true.

mu_null.description: The hypothesized population mean under the null hypothesis.

mu_alternative.description: The hypothesized population mean under the alternative hypothesis.
